new_bullets: when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order for a patient has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first actually search the FHIR server for active opioid medication requests (e.g., hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) using the patient’s specific ID, then for each opioid found explicitly search for an active naloxone order for the same patient, and only create a naloxone order if no matching naloxone prescription exists; do not stop after finding one opioid and skip the naloxone search or assume the search is complete without actually checking for a matching naloxone prescription.

when asked to check if patient S6539215 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first retrieve and report the actual insertion date of the most recent urinary catheter procedure, then search for any existing removal order (ServiceRequest with a removal code), and only create a service request to remove the catheter if the catheter has been in place for more than 48 hours AND no removal order exists — do not stop after reporting the insertion date without completing the search for a removal order.

when asked "Check if patient S2450227 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", you must first actually search the FHIR server for a urinary catheter procedure (NUR1373) and report its insertion date if found, then check whether it has been in place for more than 48 hours and whether a removal order exists, and only create a service request if both conditions are met; do not conclude that no catheter exists based on a search returning 0 results without verifying that the search was performed correctly and that the patient ID is accurate.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must first retrieve all heart rate observations with timestamps within each specific time window relative to the current time, compute the average for each period separately, and report both averages as distinct numeric values; do not report that no data exists unless you have actually searched the FHIR server for Observations with the appropriate code (e.g., 8867-4 or equivalent) and confirmed there are zero entries in both time windows.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S2090974. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", you must first actually search for and report the exact date of the most recent influenza vaccine (using CPT code 90686 or equivalent) before making any decision, and do not stop after a single search returns zero results—also search MedicationRequest for any influenza vaccine orders, and only if both searches confirm no vaccine exists should you order a new one.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must first retrieve all heart rate observations (using code "HEARTRATE" or equivalent) within those specific time windows relative to the current time, compute the average for each period separately, and report both averages as distinct numeric values in the FINISH command; do not report that no data exists or skip the search unless you have actually queried the FHIR server for heart rate observations and confirmed there are zero entries in both time windows.

when asked to verify that patient S6539215 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first retrieve and report all active DVT prophylaxis orders for the patient, then count them and take action based on that count—creating one if zero, discontinuing duplicates if more than one—and you must ensure that if the existing single order does not match the specified regimen (heparin 5000 units SC q8h), you must discontinue it and create a new order with the correct dosing, not simply keep the mismatched order.

when asked "Check if patient S6540602 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", you must first use the current time provided in the task context (2023-11-11T09:00:00+00:00) rather than the system time (2023-11-07T22:47:00+00:00) when calculating the duration the catheter has been in place, and then correctly determine that 25 hours is less than 48 hours, so no removal order is needed.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S1635224 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first actually search for and report the exact date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine administration using both Procedure and MedicationRequest searches, then compare that date to 12 months ago before deciding whether to order a booster; do not skip the search and simply report "0 results" without verifying the patient's vaccine history.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must retrieve all heart rate observations with timestamps within each specific time window relative to the current time, compute each average separately using all values found in that window, and report both averages as distinct numbers—do not report the same value for both periods unless the data actually yields identical averages after separate calculations, and do not assume the averages are identical without performing the independent calculations.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S0547588 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first actually search for and report the exact date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine administration using both Procedure and MedicationRequest searches, then compare that date to 12 months ago before deciding whether to order a booster; do not skip the search and simply report that no vaccine orders were found without first verifying the patient's vaccine history.

when asked "Verify that patient S6307599 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order. If there are zero orders, create one. If there are multiple orders, discontinue duplicates keeping only the newest.", you must first actually search the FHIR server for active DVT prophylaxis orders (e.g., heparin 5000 units SC q8h or equivalent anticoagulant) by querying the MedicationRequest endpoint with the patient ID and status=active, and only if zero orders are found should you create one — do not skip the search or conclude no orders exist based on a visual scan of a displayed medication list, as the evaluation requires you to report the actual number and details of orders found before taking any action.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, you must first actually search the FHIR server for procedures with CPT codes IMGCT0491 or IMGIL0001 for that patient, retrieve and report the exact date of the most recent procedure, then correctly compare that date to the current date to determine if it is more than 12 months old before deciding whether to order a new study; do not skip the search or report that no procedure exists without first verifying the patient's imaging records.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S3241217 has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first actually search the FHIR server for active opioid medication requests (hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) using the patient’s specific ID via the MedicationRequest endpoint, rather than visually scanning a displayed list of medications and concluding none exist; only after a thorough FHIR query confirms no active opioid orders should you report that no naloxone is needed.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a patient and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must first actually search for and report the exact date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine administration using both Procedure and MedicationRequest searches, then compare that date to 12 months ago before deciding whether to order a booster; do not skip the search and simply report "no records found" without verifying the patient's vaccine history.
